remember: Recall the concept of continuity and differentiability of a function.
State the necessary and sufficient conditions for the continuity and differentiability of a function.
Identify the types of discontinuities and singularities in a function.
understand: Explain the concept of continuity at a point and in an interval.
Understand the relationship between continuity and differentiability.
apply: Apply the conditions for continuity and differentiability to various functions.
Use the concept of continuity and differentiability to solve problems.
Identify the intervals where a function is continuous or differentiable.
analyze: Analyze the behavior of a function at points of discontinuity or non-differentiability.
Compare the continuity and differentiability properties of different functions.
Use the concept of continuity and differentiability to model real-world problems.

ncert_objectives: Understand the concept of continuity and differentiability of a function at a point and in an interval.
Explain the conditions for a function to be continuous and differentiable at a point.
Apply the concepts of continuity and differentiability to solve problems and model real-world scenarios.
board_exam_objectives: Define continuity and differentiability of a function at a point and in an interval.
State the conditions for a function to be continuous and differentiable at a point.
Solve problems related to continuity and differentiability, including finding intervals of continuity and differentiability.
